entry.title: Re: SAP Community Hobbies Series: Two Hearts, Four Paws: Growing Together Through Dog Agility
entry.content:
Hi Chilli and @smarchesini  thank you so much for your lovely message! 
I’m really happy you could feel the love and passion behind our story.

Yes, in competitions there are different categories, usually based on the dog’s size and on experience levels (grades). This helps make things fair and fun for everyone, both for the dogs and the handlers.

In principle, almost any dog can practice agility, not only “sporty” breeds. What really matters is that the dog is healthy, enjoys it, and that the training is adapted to their abilities and limits. For us, fun and well-being always come first.

For Joker, the most difficult obstacle has been the seesaw (the bascule), because at the beginning the noise when it hits the ground scared him a bit. But with patience and training, he improved a lot and now he handles it much better 😊

A big hello to Chilli, the “lazy dog”

entry.title: How to assign role to communication user in s/4hana public edition?
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have created a communication arrangement of scenario SAP_COM_0120 in a partner version of S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition. A communication user is created and used for inbound communication under this arrangement. 

However, I developed a ui5 app (in my trial BTP account) to consume the odata v2 service of a API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV. But this app failed to load resource. I guess the access to an API in this arrangement failed due to insufficient authorization.  I have traversed many apps, display communication scenarios, communication arrangements, maintain communication users and communication systems. None of them enables  the role assignment to communication user. 

The screenshot of the returned XML has been uploaded as attachment among others, including communication user and arrangement.

Is it possible that I need a BTP account of the same Public Cloud system to enable the access to the relevant APIs?  I registered this public cloud system in my BTP trial account and created a destination for it. If the App were developed in the same system,  there won't be a status of 404? 

Could anyone drop me some clue?

entry.title: Announcing SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 28th February 2026
entry.content:
Dear Community Members,

We are excited to invite you to SAP Inside Track (SIT) Bengaluru on October 5th, 2024! This year, we are offering an exclusive opportunity to explore the future of SAP Business AI—the forefront of innovation.

Join us as we delve into the world of Business AI: learn how to build AI solutions on SAP BTP, discover how partners and customers are leveraging SAP Business AI, and explore various SAP solutions with embedded AI through hands-on, insightful sessions and demos.

Event Overview

SAP Inside Track 2024 – Business AI Edition aims to provide CXOs, Leaders, Developers, Consultants, Architects, and IT Managers with a deep understanding of how to effectively integrate Business AI with SAP products.

This event is your gateway to unlocking the full potential of SAP and AI within your organization.

What is SAP Inside Track?

SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, and leaders gather to share knowledge and insights on SAP-related topics. Organized by members of the SAP Community, it provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise.
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here!

Event Details:  

Date: February 28th, 2026
Time: 8 AM - 5 PM IST 
Venue:  Auditorium, SAP Labs India Innovation Park, Bande Kodigehalli, Singahalli, Karnataka 562149Bengaluru, India

Registration:  

Don't miss out on this incredible opportunity to be part of Bengaluru's largest SAP community event!

Register Now!
Note: This is an in-person event only.

Agenda:  

This SAP Inside Track will be a full-day exploration of SAP Business AI. You will:

  • Learn to build AI-powered applications on SAP Business Technology Platform.
  • Discover innovative Business AI solutions from leading partners.
  • Witness the transformative power of Embedded AI through live demos across SAP solutions.

Our event features expert-led sessions by SAP, partners, and customers. Plus, a dedicated hands-on workshop zone and demo booths showcasing AI use cases.

Stay tuned for the full agenda, which will be posted soon! Join the SAP Community Bengaluru group to stay updated!

Speaker Registration:

Speaker registrations are now open! If you're an expert in building AI applications on SAP BTP, developing innovative Business AI solutions, or working with embedded AI in SAP solutions like SAP S/4HANA or SuccessFactors, we welcome your contribution. We also invite partners and customers to showcase their work.
Register as a speaker here!

Exclusive Sponsorship Opportunities:

With over 4,000 community members across locations in the past 12 months, we’re eager to continue growing. If you're interested in sponsoring the event, please comment below, and we’ll reach out to you. This year, we have exclusive sponsor bundles that offer opportunities to showcase your content and expertise across multiple locations, including Bangalore, Gurgaon, Hyderabad, and Pune.

Join the Bengaluru SAP Community Group:

If you're in Bengaluru or Karnataka, join the Bengaluru SAP Community Group to engage in discussions, share ideas, and collaborate with fellow SAP enthusiasts.
Stay connected with us on Twitter and LinkedIn for updates on this and future community events.

Best Regards,
Ranjeet

entry.title: 3rd SAP Stammtisch in Cyprus: 100 Years of Experience and 5 Key Takeaways
entry.content:
What a great session! Last week, the third SAP Stammtisch here in Cyprus brought together an amazing group of people once again. Great atmosphere, open exchange, and above all, concentrated expert knowledge. We did a quick calculation and realized we had about 100 years of collective SAP experience at the table.

Our topic of the evening was S/4HANA Transformation. We asked ourselves: what are the absolute essentials to make this project a success? The result of our discussion is these 5 central takeaways:

1. Collaboration and Communication S/4HANA is not just an IT project. Silos must be broken down. Business and IT need to speak a common language; otherwise, development will miss the actual requirements.

2. Commitment and Clear Decisions Agile is good, but a lack of commitment is fatal. You need a strong mandate and decisions that stick so the project can gain real momentum.

3. Process Design, Documentation, and Simplification No pure "lift and shift"! The transformation is a chance to cut off old habits. Moving "Back to Standard" only works through radical simplification of processes.

4. Change Management and Enablement The best technology is useless if the users don't get on board. Change must be managed professionally through training, communication, and real empowerment of key users.

5. Master Data Quality and Data Cleansing The classic issue that is often addressed too late. "Garbage in, garbage out" applies here more than ever. Without massive data cleansing beforehand, the go-live will be rocky.

A huge thank you to all participants — Damla Toraman, Feyzi Göktas, Eugen Lust, Igor Perelmann, Raphael Klosek, Sabine Clemens — for this inspiring evening! It proves once again that nothing replaces personal exchange.

Which of these points do you see as the biggest stumbling block?

#SAP #S4HANA #SAPCommunity #Cyprus #Transformation #ERP #Networking

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - APIM - Domain-Centric Routing Pattern (DCRP): Governing APIs via CPI Provider - Part I
entry.content:
@ATU 

Thank you very much for comment.

The main trade-off is between centralized governance (security, compliance, observability) and domain team autonomy for rapid changes. DCRP solves this with clear separation — centralized APIM manages the contract (routing rules, policies — changes quarterly), while domain teams deploy iFlows and update KVM autonomously (changes weekly). Adding a new vendor = 30 seconds (1 KVM line), zero proxy changes. In practice, organizations vary: Banking/Pharma favor strong governance (max security, moderate speed); Startups prefer high autonomy (max speed, controlled risk); Enterprises adopt hybrid (centralized APIM + decentralized CPI = balance between governance and agility).

This pattern originated from deep study of the standard Dynamic Routing Pattern in API Management and is the result of a proof-of-concept implementation. In Part III (currently in progress), I'll provide the full framework for PoC implementation in 30 minutes — no modifications to policies or KVMs required, just testing. Scaling this pattern depends entirely on discipline, organization, and governance.

Stu Ztu: "governance should enable agile delivery within clear policies, not delay it".

The Commander | Viana.

In the above screenshot, we are deleting the condition valid from 01.05.2028 to 31.12.9999 and system also flags the condition 01.01.2026 to 01.31.2026 for deletion. Both these condition has the same amount as price. The condition type is a copy of PR00 condition type. 

The only connection i can think off is, originally the condition ending on 31.12.9999 had the starting date as 01.01.2026 and it was moved to 01.05.2026 as the other conditions were added. 

Appreciate your help

entry.title: system Picks Only One Freight Order Type When Planning Different FU Types based on condition det.
entry.content:
We are using capacity doc. creation with the condition /SCMTMS/TOR_TYPE in the planning profile to determine the Freight Order (FO) type based on Freight Unit (FU) attributes (e.g., FU Type, DG, Origin Country).

Observed behavior in manual and automatic assign/ assign selected items in cockpit:

  • When planning a single FU the FO type determination works correctly.
  • When planning multiple FUs with different FU types, the system combines them into one FO and applies one FO type (typically from the first FU).
    ➜ This is wrong for our process; each FU type should lead to its own FO type.

Tried already

  • Verified /SCMTMS/TOR_TYPE condition in planning profile.
  • Tested with VSR and manual planning.
  • Maintained FU–FU incompatibility (split by FU Type / DG).
    → System shows a warning, but still allows saving one FO (no auto-split, no separate FO types).

Requirement

  • When selecting multiple FUs with different FU types, the system must automatically create separate FOs, each with the correct FO type according to /SCMTMS/TOR_TYPE.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Steps by Step Guide to enable Joule in Fiori launchpad for S4HANA 2025 Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Hi 
Great blog. However, a few things seem to be missing.
We had Joule running on S/4HANA 2023 PCE (still in PoC phase), but after the upgrade to 2025, Joule stopped working across all environments (DEV, TST, and PRD)

During our analysis, we discovered a new parameter that appears to be undocumented yet.

DAS_WEBCLIENT_PLUGIN


This parameter is Activated by default and contains following URL https://q2c-joule-pilot-multi-system.eu12.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4/

The thing is, that isn't our subaccount; it seems to be a demo account. (Maybe it's a version of Joule that allows connecting more than one system? The name suggests so.)

After the upgrade, this parameter now covers the Technical Catalog settings by default

And as a result the following error occurs 

Notes 3652218 - "Refused to frame" error message when using Joule in FLP  and 3673511 - Troubleshooting for Joule page display issues when opening Joule in SAP products do not resolve the issue because we don't have access to this subaccount.
Official Joule documentation (https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf) doesn't mention about.

And there are two possible solutions for this.

1. Update DAS_WEBCLIENT_PLUGIN with our Joule URL

2. Remove DAS_WEBCLIENT_PLUGIN parameter.

I have raised a ticket to OSS for this. 
Artur

entry.title: Re: Timesheet completeness validation
entry.content:
Dear @vasco_keizer,

1. Standard SAP option for Project Managers (pre-billing control)

Notify Missing Times (F2556) is the standard way for Project Management to check whether team members time is up to date missing time before starting billing proposal / billing preparation and to follow up with consultants

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2556')/S36

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003525043

2. Standard automation (reduce manual chasing)

If you want a systematic everyone must be complete by X day mechanism, SAP provides a standard scheduled job template to send automated emails for missing time

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/f3be0d588d9ab244e10000000a4450e5.html

There is also a standard monitoring app commonly used to track reminder notifications, Monitor Missing Time Notifications (F3031)

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/change-viewing-rights-for-fiori-app-quot-monitor-missing-time-notifications/qaq-p/12414729

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3031')/S36

3. When you need a global completeness dashboard

If your requirement is an enterprise-level view like recorded hours + leave vs planned working time work schedule/availability per employee/week then a reporting/dashboard layer is often the best approach in Public Cloud

SAP-supported building blocks for reporting:

  • Timesheet reporting CDS references: I_TimeSheet and I_TimeSheetRecord

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003495527

  • Availability/target-hours side and related checks: I_TimeOverviewByWrkAgrmtAndDte and API guidance API_MANAGE_WF_AVAILABILITY

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003654922

  • IMPORTANT: Missing time can be false positive/negative if target hours/availability isn’t maintained correctly

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003391722

Summary:

  • Operational PM gate before billing use Notify Missing Times (F2556) per project/team
  • Automation schedule the Missing Time Entries email notification job weekly or multiple times per week
  • Enterprise transparency (optional) build an Embedded Analytics dashboard using released CDS objects to show missing hours by consultant/week and optionally drill down by project

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Automated Exporting and downloading of report to local shared folder
entry.content:
Hi everyone! 👋

I'm working on automating the download of certain reports from SAP to a shared network folder on my PC, and I've hit a small roadblock. Hoping someone here has dealt with this before!

What we've done so far:

  • ✅ Created a shared folder with read and write access
  • ✅ Had SAP whitelist the shared folder IP
  • ✅ Developed a custom Z-program and tested it
  • ✅ Getting "downloading successfully" message

The issue: Despite the success message, no files are actually appearing in the shared folder 

What we found: After some research, it seems we need to:

  • Mount the IP address to the application server
  • Configure the path as a directory in AL11

My question: Has anyone faced a similar situation? If so, could you please walk me through the exact steps needed to get this working? We're running S/4HANA Private Cloud.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated! Thanks in advance! 🙏

entry.title: Re: Turning on "Lexicographical" setting for Product masters
entry.content: This restriction is by design in S/4HANA Cloud. Once numeric material numbers exist in a tenant, SAP does not allow the lexicographical indicator to be switched on, even if those materials are archived or inactive. Unfortunately, SAP will not enable this setting manually in Production either, as SSCUI 102320 is treated as a system-critical configuration and must be consistent across tenants. Since you haven’t started activities in PROD yet, the safest path is to enable lexicographical numbering before any material creation, or consider a tenant refresh once the legacy constraints are cleared. I’d strongly recommend raising an incident with SAP to confirm options, but based on current behavior, waiting or refreshing is usually the only supported route.

entry.title: Re: How do you manipulate the values of STXH, COBL, VBPA during Sales order creation or VA01?
entry.content: For VA01 you normally don’t change those tables directly. Use SD user exits or BAdIs instead. For sales order data and partners (VBPA), check the exits in MV45AFZZ. For accounting-related fields (COBL / STXH), the values are set later, so a BAdI like SD_ACCOUNTING is usually the right place. Updating the tables directly isn’t supported.

entry.title: The Fortress of Modern Commerce: Why RISE with SAP is the Answer to the "Digital Siege"
entry.content:
Fortress of Modern Commerce: Why RISE with SAP is the Answer to the "Digital Siege"

As a leader for SAP Enterprise Cloud Services, I spend my days talking to executives who share a common, growing anxiety: "Are we truly protected?"

In recent months, the global manufacturing landscape has been shaken by the events at Jaguar Land Rover. It wasn't just a data leak; it was a full-scale "digital siege" that halted production for weeks. While many focus on the "how," the industry knows the "why": a critical failure in patch management. This is a stark reminder: in a hyper-connected world, an unpatched system is an open door.

The Stakes: Half of the World’s GDP

When we talk about RISE with SAP, we aren't just talking about "moving to the cloud." We are talking about the engine of the global economy.

Did you know? Approximately 77% of the world’s transaction revenue touches an SAP system. On any given day, RISE with SAP facilitates transactions equivalent to half of the global GDP.

If SAP stops, the world stops. This is why our security posture isn't just "industry standard"—it is mission-critical infrastructure.

The Patch Management Advantage: Closing the Window of Vulnerability

The JLR incident highlighted a universal truth: hackers don't always "break in"; they "log in" through vulnerabilities that should have been closed months prior.

In a traditional on-premise or unmanaged setup, the responsibility for patching lies with your internal teams, who are often stretched thin. With RISE with SAP Private Cloud, we have never been breached precisely because we eliminate that "window of vulnerability."

We don't just host; we automate the hygiene. From the OS to the database, SAP manages the lifecycle of updates so that a "known vulnerability" never becomes your "business outage."

In the Room: A Conversation with a Concerned CIO

Here is how I discuss this proactive defense with our clients:

Client: "We saw the JLR fallout. It seems their down-time was a result of delayed patches. How does RISE prevent that same human error or delay in our organization?"

Me: "That’s the core of the value proposition. In your current environment, patching is a project you have to schedule. With RISE, it’s a service we deliver. We take the 'human' delay out of the equation by maintaining a rigorous, automated patch cycle. We handle the technical debt so you don't have to worry about a patch being missed because a team was too busy with a rollout."

Client: "But can we really trust a third party with that level of control?"

Me: "Think of it this way: We manage 50% of the world's GDP. Our survival depends on our ability to keep those systems secure. To this day, the SAP Private Cloud perimeter remains undefeated because our dedicated Security Operations Centers (SOC) treat a single missing patch as a critical threat. We close the doors before the hackers even reach the gate."

Resilience as a Competitive Advantage

Digital resilience is the new gold standard. Moving to RISE with SAP is an insurance policy for your brand's reputation. By standardizing your core and handing the management of security updates to the experts, you move from a reactive posture to a proactive one.

Next Steps

The question is no longer if an attack will happen, but how fast your defenses can adapt. Are you still manually managing your security gates, or are you in a managed cloud designed to withstand the storm?

entry.title: Project Resource Demand - Service Activity Type Actualization & Revaluation of Projects
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP gurus, 

   Please forgive me if I'm missing something terribly simple, my client is going to use Cloud Public 2602 and I'm trying to discover the newly introduced functionalities and would like to clear one thing. In EPPM the design expectation is using the project structures in profile projects with revenues.

   I've created a project and a WBS, an internal resource demand for the WBS, assigned resource to a worker ( WBS, worker assigned to the same cost center), maintained worker's service level plan cost rate and spend some time to the project via My Timesheet application successfully. 

   I can monitor the costs by (allocated time * plan cost rate) in WBS, I also configured one of the rule line of the predefined splitting structure for the specific activity on service level as well. Actual cost rate calculation has been done and I could see the acual cost rate in "Manage Cost Rates - Actual" application, the output is consistent.

   Before all, I got to mention that I've changed the activity type main data as follows : Default Variance Values for Actual Allocation ->  Actual Price Indicator -> From none to 5 "Actual price, automatically based on activity". I know how material ledger handles the variances and revaluate the activity consumption but here I made an assumption that there might be some sort of a layer similar to ML which allows same functionality. Still, I went to actual costing run and wanted to observe if the activity is selected but the specific service activity is not selected as supposed.

   The question is, is it possible to handle the service activity p/a variances as a period end process and allocate them to the WBS in Cloud Public 2602 ? My clients expectation is monitoring the plan activities through the period and allocate the variances after calculated from "service" cost centers. In my point of view, there is no such layer or function to fullfill this expectation in standard however sort of work arounds can be applied such as manual distribution of variances to the projects by Universal Alloc. with rec. factor of these activities period end quantities.

   Thanks in advance for your interest and guidance.

entry.title: Re: Learning expiration email notification setup
entry.content:
Hi

It is not possible to setup a stagerred number of days at the global APM level to trigger Expiration and Overdue emails. It is driven off the initial and the threshold days.

You may look at customising this at the item level if required - but that will still follow the initial and the threshold day format.

Thank you

entry.title: ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINTにおける「FOR <sql_statement>」の指定方法について
entry.content:
現在、現行環境と比較して新環境の処理時間が遅く、業務に支障が生じており、
新環境を本番稼働できない可能性があるという大きな課題があります。
 
このため、処理時間が遅いSQL文に対してHINT句を指定する方法について調査を行っています。
 
SAP Note DIGITS の「6. 文のヒントはどのように管理できますか。」にて、
以下のSQLコマンドの説明があることを確認しました。
ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT ( <hints> ) [OVERRIDE] [COMMENT '<comment>'] FOR <sql_statement>
 
本コマンドについて、以下の２点をご教示いただけますでしょうか。
 
１．FOR <sql_statement> の <sql_statement> には、STATEMENT HASH ではなく、SQL文そのものを指定することは可能でしょうか。
 
２．（上記 1 において SQL文を指定可能な場合）
　　<sql_statement> に、以下の例のように % などのワイルドカードを指定することは可能でしょうか。
　　目的としては、1回のコマンドで複数のSQL文に対してHINT句を指定できるかを確認したいと考えています。
 
　　ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT ( USE_HEX_PLAN ) FOR 'SELECT % FROM "Z_CDS_VIEW_A" WHERE %'
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINTにおける「FOR <sql_statemen...
entry.content:
＞１．FOR <sql_statement> の <sql_statement> には、STATEMENT HASH ではなく、SQL文そのものを指定することは可能でしょうか。
▼
ご認識通りになります。
 
＞２．<sql_statement> に、以下の例のように % などのワイルドカードを指定することは可能でしょうか。
▼
不可になります。
<sql_statement> を対象SQL文の全文で指定する必要があります。

・参考資料
2142945 - FAQ: SAP HANA Hints
2400006 - FAQ: SAP HANA Statement Hints

entry.title: Integration Suite issue with Deep Entity create: dependent entities missing
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing a rather interesting issue that I have never seen before. I have the following scenario: An integration flow in Integration Suite that connects to an OData service on-premise, with cloud connector setup (the Odata service is hosted on premise, ECC.

 

All is well but for one flow: I have an scenario that i will forward a payload like this one:

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><TransportEntitySet><TransportEntity><CodigoRota>15573914</CodigoRota><DataHoraImportacaoRota>2026-01-30T11:28:00</DataHoraImportacaoRota><DataHoraInicioRota>2026-01-30T16:22:00</DataHoraInicioRota><DataHoraFimRota>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimRota><NomeRota>0060012623</NomeRota><Status>2</Status><IdDeposito/><Entregas><Entrega><CodigoEntrega>118350098</CodigoEntrega><DataHoraChegada>2026-01-30T16:22:00</DataHoraChegada><DataHoraFimAtendimento>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimAtendimento><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352640</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352640</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794518</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Lorem Ipsum</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos></Entrega></Entregas><Entregas><Entrega><CodigoEntrega>118350099</CodigoEntrega><DataHoraChegada>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraChegada><DataHoraFimAtendimento>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimAtendimento><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352641</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352641</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794520</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Texto 01 / Texto 02 / Texto 03 / Texto 04</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352642</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352642</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794521</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Texto 01 / Texto 02 / Texto 03 / Texto 04</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos></Entrega></Entregas></TransportEntity></TransportEntitySet>

As the OData adapter only accepts XML for POST methods, we'll send the payload as above as the result of the conversion of a JSON to XML, so looking at the XML seems correct. The definition of the service is as below (from the associations panel):

 

 

However when the OData adapter is called, the message is transformed to the following payload, where each of the composite (expanded) entities Entregas and Pedidos have only one entry, namely the last one.

I Thought it was a cardinality problem, but it doesn't seem like that. I'll try to assign referential constraints based on the id of the message but it should not have any.

Has anyone faced something similar? i think i'll reshape the service and maybe add some referential constraints

appreciate any inputs

Erick

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP-CPI-Package-Domain-Centric-Pattern (PDCP):Solving Package Sprawl at Scale-PART II
entry.content:
Thanks for the detailed write-up. I have some concerns about this approach from both practical implementation and presentation perspectives:

1. Language & Cultural Sensitivity The heavy use of military metaphors throughout ("Commander," "battlefield," "blast radius control," "surgical precision," "operational theater") is problematic. Given the current global situation with ongoing conflicts affecting millions of people, this language feels tone-deaf and inappropriate for technical documentation. Integration architecture is not warfare—it's collaborative problem-solving. Professional technical content should use neutral, inclusive language that resonates globally across diverse audiences. Terms like "mitigation strategy," "impact isolation," or "failure containment" convey the same technical concepts without the militaristic framing.

2. Complexity vs. Value Introducing SAP API Management as a default proxy layer in front of SAP CI adds significant complexity, cost, and maintenance overhead. For many organizations, direct SAP CI endpoints with proper security are sufficient. The DCRP+PDCP synergy may be over-engineered for most use cases.

3. Package Grouping Philosophy The domain-centric consolidation (39 packages → 4) addresses a real problem, but the solution may be too rigid. Traditional grouping by third-party system or specific business process often works better in SAP CI's architecture, which differs fundamentally from SAP PO. One Salesforce package, one Shopify package, etc., with clear naming conventions, provides good isolation and maintainability without forcing everything into four buckets.

4. Technical User Management The concern about credential management (39 users → 12) is overstated. Modern credential management tools (CyberArk, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault) handle this well, and separate users per package provide better security isolation, clearer audit trails, and proper separation of concerns. The "credential nightmare" scenario assumes poor tooling and manual processes that shouldn't exist in 2026.

5. Self-Proclamation Declaring oneself a "SAP BTP Integration Suite Expert" and "The Commander" undermines credibility. Let the technical content demonstrate expertise rather than relying on self-assigned titles.

Core Value The naming convention framework (iFlow DNA) and focus on reducing package sprawl through standardization are valuable contributions. However, the architectural pattern may be significantly more complex than necessary for most organizations.

A simpler, proven approach:

  • Enforce clear naming standards across all iFlows
  • Group packages logically by third-party system or bounded business process
  • Use modern credential management tools with automated rotation
  • Add API Management only when there's a clear business case (external partner access, rate limiting, monetization)
  • Avoid premature optimization—start simple, add complexity only when justified

Would be interested in hearing from others who've implemented this pattern at scale and whether the operational benefits justified the implementation complexity.

entry.title: Re: End of Support indirect quotations BYOR (1S4)
entry.content:
Dear Janernst,

Thank you for reaching out with your concerns regarding the upcoming 2602 release and its impact on the BYOR (Bring Your Own Rates) solution for scope item 1S4.

Following a thorough confirmation with our development team, we would like to clarify the following points to address your questions:

First and foremost, please be assured that the indirect quotation functionality for the BYOR solution will remain available in the 2602 release. There is no plan to completely disable this feature at this time.

Additionally, we would like to highlight that for the European Central Bank (ECB), direct quotation data is also available. Please refer to the attached screenshot:

Best Regards,

Lawrence

entry.title: Re: How to Build Stock Validation Skills in Joule Studio
entry.content:
In Joule Skill workflows, is there any way to create a custom function to process input parameters?

Currently, it seems that input parameters can only be handled as String values. If a user provides input such as “process document 1,2,3,4”, is it possible to implement a custom function to convert that String into a List of Strings and then pass it to an API?

Are there any built-in capabilities or recommended approaches to handle this kind of input transformation within Joule Skills?

entry.title: Re: The Fortress of Modern Commerce: Why RISE with SAP is the Answer to the "Digital Siege&quot
entry.content: Responsibilities for patching are shared between SAP and customers in RISE. SAP is responsible for infrastructure and Basis patches. However, application security notes require the input of customers

entry.title: Installation Success Factor xxxxxxx not visible via create OSS Case
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

we have done the hyperscaler move last week for our Success Factor instances. 

Therefore their Installation Number, Customer number and system number has changed. 

Since the move, we do see new instances under Installations, but we are not able to  find them via System Overview in opening of oss case. 

We have checked the authorisation and it is correct. Nobody can find these systems - regardless of given authorisation. 

We have noticed in addition, that we are not able to  add new customer number under Display Cloud data authorization. We see here only old ZIPCODE Customer number. 

Could you please check from your side what is missing? 

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Installation Success Factor xxxxxxx not visible via c...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If you are not able to create case for the new SF installation, then very likely your S-user is not the cloud admin for that SF installation and have no authorization 'Display Cloud Data' assigned on that SF installation number.

Could you pls kindly check via the My Important Contact page and find the current active cloud admin for that SF installation number?

Then pls kindly contact the corresponding SF cloud admin to get the authorization 'Display Cloud Data' assigned on that SF installation number.

Check KBA 2598170 - What is the Display Cloud Data authorization? to know more about this authorization.

In case you have issue to find the correct SF installation or you still cannot create SF case after getting the required authorization, feel free to create a case using component XX-SER-FORME.

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support
